title_en: Al-Juwayni: The Imam of the Two Holy Sanctuaries
title_ar: Al-Juwayni: The Imam of the Two Holy Sanctuaries
content_en:
Abu al-Ma'ali Abd al-Malik ibn Abdallah al-Juwayni was born in Juwayn, Khurasan, in 419 AH (1028 CE) and died in Nishapur in 478 AH (1085 CE). Known by the honorific al-Imam al-Haramayn — the Imam of the Two Holy Sanctuaries — a title he earned from his years teaching in Mecca and Medina after leaving his homeland to avoid political difficulties associated with the Ash'ari school's relationship with the ruling authorities, al-Juwayni was the most important Ash'ari theologian of the fifth Islamic century and the teacher of al-Ghazali.

Al-Juwayni's intellectual formation placed him at the apex of the Ash'ari tradition as it had developed through al-Ash'ari himself, al-Baqillani, Ibn Furak, and others. He was also deeply engaged with Shafi'i jurisprudence and wrote major works in usul al-fiqh (legal theory). His combination of excellence in theology, legal theory, and jurisprudence made him the model of the comprehensive Sunni scholar that the Nizamiyyah madrasa system — founded by the Seljuk vizier Nizam al-Mulk — aimed to produce.

Al-Irshad ila Qawati' al-Adillah fi Usul al-I'tiqad — The Guide to the Conclusive Proofs for the Principles of Belief — is al-Juwayni's mature systematic exposition of Ash'ari theology. It represents the tradition at a more advanced and technically demanding level than introductory creedal summaries, engaging in detail with the philosophical and theological arguments that had been developed over centuries of kalam discourse. The work is addressed to students who had already mastered basic theology and were ready to engage with its more complex theoretical dimensions.

Al-Irshad's title is significant: it promises guidance (irshad) to proofs that are conclusive (qawati' al-adillah) — not merely probable or conventional but genuinely demonstrative. This reflects al-Juwayni's aspiration for a theology that achieves the certainty of demonstration rather than merely the plausibility of persuasion. Whether he fully realized this aspiration was debated by later scholars, including al-Ghazali, who was more skeptical about the demonstrative power of kalam arguments.
content_id:
Abu al-Ma'ali Abd al-Malik ibn Abd Allah al-Juwayni (419–478 H / 1028–1085 M), yang dikenal dengan gelar kehormatan Imam al-Haramayn (Imam dua Tanah Suci), adalah salah satu teolog Muslim terbesar dalam tradisi Asy'ariyyah. Ia menghabiskan bertahun-tahun mengajar di Mekah dan Madinah sebelum kembali ke Nishapur, kota kelahirannya di Persia.

Al-Irshad ila Qawati' al-Adillah fi Usul al-I'tiqad (Panduan Menuju Argumen-Argumen Penentu dalam Pokok-Pokok Akidah) ditulis al-Juwayni sebagai teks sistematis dalam teologi kalam Asy'ariyyah. Karya ini merangkum metodologi dan kesimpulan-kesimpulan teologi Asy'ariyyah dalam bentuk yang lebih ringkas dan lebih mudah dipahami dibandingkan karya-karya teologi kalam sebelumnya.

Al-Juwayni adalah murid dari Abu Ishaq al-Isfaraini dan generasi penerus langsung dari Abu Bakr al-Baqillani, yang keduanya adalah pilar-pilar utama mazhab Asy'ariyyah setelah pendirinya, Abu al-Hasan al-Asy'ari (260–324 H). Beliau kemudian menjadi guru dari salah satu tokoh paling berpengaruh dalam sejarah Islam: Abu Hamid al-Ghazali (ra).

Tradisi kalam Asy'ariyyah — yang menjadi latar Al-Irshad — berupaya mempertahankan akidah Islam dari serangan filosofis menggunakan perangkat logika dan argumen rasional. Para teolog Asy'ariyyah berpendapat bahwa akal dapat membuktikan keberadaan Allah, keesaan-Nya, dan kebutuhan akan para rasul, sementara wahyu memberikan rincian tentang sifat-sifat Allah dan kewajiban-kewajiban agama.

Al-Irshad membahas topik-topik utama teologi Islam: pembuktian eksistensi Allah melalui argumen kebaruan alam (huduts al-alam), sifat-sifat Allah dan bagaimana memahaminya secara rasional, persoalan kalam Allah (apakah ia nafsiy atau lafdhy), kenabian dan cara memverifikasi kebenaran seorang nabi, serta persoalan Imamah dan kepemimpinan politik umat Islam.

Karya ini memiliki pengaruh besar dalam tradisi keilmuan Islam, terutama di dunia Melayu-Indonesia, di mana teologi Asy'ariyyah menjadi akidah mayoritas. Memahami Al-Irshad berarti memahami fondasi intelektual dari tradisi kalam yang telah membentuk pemikiran teologis Islam selama berabad-abad.
content_ar:
